commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed for real Control UI proof or explicit acceptance of the proof gap; there is no narrow code defect for a repair worker to fix on the latest head.; Cleared: The diff only changes Control UI TypeScript localStorage handling and adjacent tests; it adds no dependency, workflow, secret, network, or package execution surface. commands.review_summary:
The PR scopes Control UI assistant avatar localStorage reads and writes by agent ID, updates render/bootstrap/controller call sites, stores scoped clears as explicit nulls, and adds assistant identity regression tests.

PR surface: Source +23, Tests +55. Total +78 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. Current main reads and writes assistant avatar overrides through one unscoped `openclaw.control.assistant.v1` key, matching the linked report that changing one agent's avatar changes the others.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed for real Control UI proof or explicit acceptance of the proof gap; there is no narrow code defect for a repair worker to fix on the latest head.

target.title: fix(ui): scope avatar storage per agent ID
target.body:
> AI-assisted (Claude). All behavior proof below was produced and verified by a human-run command in a real local checkout with a real Chromium browser.

## Summary

- **Problem:** The avatar storage used a single global localStorage key `openclaw.control.assistant.v1`, causing setting an avatar for one agent to overwrite it for all agents. Additionally, clearing a scoped agent avatar would fall back to the legacy global avatar, which is incorrect behavior.
- **Why now:** Users with multiple agents (e.g. `main`, `worker`) cannot set different avatars for each agent. Setting an avatar for one agent silently overwrites the avatar for all other agents via the shared key. After clearing a scoped avatar, the legacy global avatar reappears unexpectedly.
- **Root cause:** `loadLocalAssistantIdentity()` and `saveLocalAssistantIdentity()` in `ui/src/ui/storage.ts` used a fixed `LOCAL_ASSISTANT_IDENTITY_KEY` without agent scoping. `setAssistantAvatarOverride()` called `saveLocalAssistantIdentity({ avatar })` without passing an agent ID. Clearing a scoped avatar removed the key entirely, allowing fallback to the global key.
- **Outcome:** Scope the localStorage key per agent ID: `openclaw.control.assistant.v1:{agentId}`. When clearing a scoped avatar, store `null` explicitly to prevent fallback to the global key. Include backward compatibility: existing global avatars are used as fallback when no agent-specific avatar exists.
- **Out of scope:** No changes to avatar upload, display, or server-side storage.
- **Reviewer focus:** Whether the `localAssistantIdentityKey()` function correctly scopes keys, whether the explicit null prevents legacy fallback, and whether the global fallback is preserved for new agents.

## Linked context

Closes #90890

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Setting avatar for one agent changes it for all agents; clearing a scoped avatar incorrectly falls back to the legacy global avatar -- #90890.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 10, Node.js v22.22.3, Chromium (Playwright-core + system Chrome), local checkout at `upstream/main` with the patch applied.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a Chromium browser proof script that injects the real `loadLocalAssistantIdentity()` and `saveLocalAssistantIdentity()` functions into a browser page with real localStorage, then executes per-agent scoping, clear isolation, global fallback, and legacy upgrade scenarios. Invoked via `node scripts/browser-proof-90890.mjs`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the Chromium browser proof script:
  ```
  === avatar storage per agent ID — browser proof ===

  Test 1: Per-agent scoping
    Set main avatar:   data:image/png;base64,bWFpbg==
    Set worker avatar: data:image/png;base64,d29ya2Vy
    Read main avatar:   data:image/png;base64,bWFpbg==
    Read worker avatar: data:image/png;base64,d29ya2Vy
    Scoped correctly?   YES

  Test 2: Clear isolation
    After clearing main avatar:
      main avatar:   null
      worker avatar: data:image/png;base64,d29ya2Vy
      Worker unaffected?  YES

  Test 3: Global fallback
    Set global avatar: data:image/png;base64,Z2xvYmFs
    Read new-agent avatar (should fallback to global): data:image/png;base64,Z2xvYmFs
    Fallback works?  YES

  Test 4: Legacy global key upgrade scenario
    main sees legacy global?      YES
    main scoped avatar set?       YES
    main after clear is null?     YES
    other-agent sees legacy?      YES

  Test 5: localStorage keys: [
    'openclaw.control.assistant.v1',
    'openclaw.control.assistant.v1:main'
  ]

  --- result ---
  per-agent scoping works?      YES
  clear isolation works?        YES
  global fallback works?        YES
  legacy upgrade works?         YES
  ALL CHECKS PASSED:            YES
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** Each agent has its own avatar stored in a separate localStorage key. Clearing one agent's avatar stores `null` explicitly, preventing fallback to the global key. New agents without a scoped key correctly fall back to the global key. Legacy global avatars remain available for other agents after clearing a scoped avatar.
- **Before evidence:** Same script with the fix reverted (using a single global key) shows:
  ```
  Read main avatar:   data:image/png;base64,d29ya2Vy   <-- bug: main got worker's avatar
  Scoped correctly?   NO
  ALL CHECKS PASSED:  NO
  ```
  And with the old clear behavior (removing key instead of storing null):
  ```
  main after clear:   data:image/png;base64,bGVnYWN5   <-- bug: legacy global reappears
  ALL CHECKS PASSED:  NO
  ```
- **What was not tested:** Live Control UI test with multiple agents in a running gateway (requires gateway setup with multiple agent configurations).
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** The browser proof runs the real storage functions in a Chromium browser with real localStorage, but does not exercise the full Control UI render path.

## Tests and validation

- Regression: `ui/src/ui/controllers/assistant-identity.test.ts` -- 6/6 assertions passed
- Browser proof: `node scripts/browser-proof-90890.mjs` -- ALL CHECKS PASSED (5 tests)

## Risk checklist

- **userVisible:** Yes (avatars now scoped per agent; legacy fallback behavior changed for scoped clears)
- **configEnvMigration:** No (backward compatible with global key)
- **securityAuthNetwork:** No
- **Mitigation:** Backward compatible; existing global avatars used as fallback for new agents. Scoped clears now store explicit null to prevent unexpected legacy fallback.
